philosophy.summary: Broad food category guides the first recommendation; ingredient families explain the details; personal history learns the final recommendation.

foodCategories.label: plain lean protein
foodCategories.reason: A simply prepared lean protein is often a more predictable starting point.

foodCategories.label: omega-3 fish
foodCategories.reason: Fish can be a useful protein and fat source, but richer fish may still depend on portion and preparation.

foodCategories.label: soft refined starch
foodCategories.reason: Soft refined starches may be physically predictable, while grain, sugar, fat, and additives still deserve a test-food rating.

foodCategories.label: simple soup or broth
foodCategories.reason: Broth and soft ingredients may be easier, but seasoning, fat, dairy, and sodium can change the result.

foodCategories.label: processed bread
foodCategories.reason: Bread is usually a middle-ground test food because texture may be soft while grains, sweeteners, oils, seeds, and preservatives vary.

foodCategories.label: processed snack
foodCategories.reason: Processed snacks usually belong in the middle because sugar, oils, dairy, wheat, flavorings, and portion size can stack up.

foodCategories.label: seasoning or sauce mix
foodCategories.reason: Seasoning and sauce mixes are test foods because hidden onion, garlic, heat, gums, sugar, acids, and sodium are common.

foodCategories.label: sauced or combination food
foodCategories.reason: Combination foods need a caution read because the base food may be simple while sauces and secondary components add risk.

foodCategories.label: dairy-rich food
foodCategories.reason: Dairy-rich foods can combine lactose and fat, making personal tolerance especially important.

foodCategories.label: fatty or processed meat
foodCategories.reason: Fatty or processed meats can be harder because fat, grease, seasoning, and processing often occur together.

foodCategories.label: fried or greasy food
foodCategories.reason: Fried and greasy foods combine a higher fat load with preparation that commonly makes symptoms harder to predict.

foodCategories.label: hulls, skins, seeds, or roughage
foodCategories.reason: Hulls, skins, seeds, and coarse roughage are higher-risk physical structures, especially during a flare.

foodCategories.label: raw or fibrous vegetable
foodCategories.reason: Raw and fibrous vegetables can be harder to break down; cooking, peeling, and portion size materially change the risk.

foodCategories.label: bean or legume dish
foodCategories.reason: Legume fiber and fermentation can make gas, bloating, and rough digestion more likely.

foodCategories.label: processed or mixed food
foodCategories.reason: A packaged food with multiple ingredients starts as a test food until its category and personal history are clearer.

ingredientFamilies.label: whole corn, kernels, or popcorn
ingredientFamilies.reason: Whole corn and popcorn contain hulls and physical structure that can be difficult to break down.

ingredientFamilies.label: processed corn ingredient
ingredientFamilies.reason: Processed corn ingredients are not the same as whole corn; they stay in the middle-ground test category.

ingredientFamilies.label: whole seeds
ingredientFamilies.reason: Small whole seeds can create a rough physical texture even when the rest of the food is soft.

ingredientFamilies.label: seed or vegetable oil
ingredientFamilies.reason: Oil is not the same physical issue as whole seeds; the concern is mainly total fat load and personal tolerance.

ingredientFamilies.label: whole nuts
ingredientFamilies.reason: Whole nut pieces may be difficult to break down because of their texture and fiber.

ingredientFamilies.label: smooth nut butter
ingredientFamilies.reason: Smooth nut butter removes most rough pieces, although fat load and individual tolerance still matter.

ingredientFamilies.label: whole or visible onion
ingredientFamilies.reason: Whole or visible onion can combine fermentable carbohydrates with physical texture and is a common personal trigger.

ingredientFamilies.label: onion powder
ingredientFamilies.reason: Powdered onion is a test ingredient: amount, texture, preparation, and personal history matter.

ingredientFamilies.label: garlic seasoning
ingredientFamilies.reason: Garlic is a common test ingredient, but it should not become an automatic Ugly rating without stronger context or personal history.

ingredientFamilies.label: mild spice or seasoning
ingredientFamilies.reason: Mild spices belong in the test category until the user's history shows whether they matter.

ingredientFamilies.label: hot pepper or strong heat
ingredientFamilies.reason: Strong heat can irritate a sensitive gut and deserves a higher-risk warning.

ingredientFamilies.label: acidic ingredient
ingredientFamilies.reason: Acidity and sharp flavorings can matter, particularly during a sensitive period or for users with reflux.

ingredientFamilies.label: milk or higher-lactose dairy
ingredientFamilies.reason: Milk, lactose, whey, and cream can cause symptoms when lactose or dairy tolerance is limited.

ingredientFamilies.label: aged or lower-lactose cheese
ingredientFamilies.reason: Aged cheese may contain less lactose, but dairy and fat tolerance still vary.

ingredientFamilies.label: butter or butter powder
ingredientFamilies.reason: Butter is a middle-ground fat and dairy ingredient, not an automatic high-risk trigger.

ingredientFamilies.label: refined wheat or flour
ingredientFamilies.reason: Refined flour is usually a test-food component: often soft, but still a grain and sometimes paired with additives.

ingredientFamilies.label: whole grain or bran
ingredientFamilies.reason: Whole grains and bran add a denser fiber matrix that can become rougher during a flare.

ingredientFamilies.label: beans or legumes
ingredientFamilies.reason: Legumes can combine dense fiber with fermentation and gas.

ingredientFamilies.label: gum or thickener
ingredientFamilies.reason: Gums and thickeners are usually small-quantity test ingredients; repeated personal outcomes matter more than one appearance.

ingredientFamilies.label: added fiber
ingredientFamilies.reason: Added fibers can increase gas or bloating and deserve more caution during a flare.

ingredientFamilies.label: sugar alcohol
ingredientFamilies.reason: Sugar alcohols commonly contribute to gas, bloating, urgency, or diarrhea.

ingredientFamilies.label: added sugar or syrup
ingredientFamilies.reason: Added sweeteners stay in the middle-ground category unless amount or personal history raises concern.

ingredientFamilies.label: preservative
ingredientFamilies.reason: Preservatives are useful tracking details but should not independently force an Ugly rating.

ingredientFamilies.label: processed meat ingredient
ingredientFamilies.reason: Processed meats can combine fat, spice, preservatives, and sodium.

ingredientFamilies.label: lean protein
ingredientFamilies.reason: A simply prepared lean protein may support a more predictable meal.

ingredientFamilies.label: fish or omega-3 protein
ingredientFamilies.reason: Fish can be a useful protein source, while richer preparations still depend on fat and portion.

nutritionRules.reason: Higher fiber can be rougher during a sensitive period.
Lower fiber can make the physical load more predictable.
A higher fat load may increase urgency or discomfort for some people.
A very high fat load deserves a stronger warning.
Higher sugar is a useful test signal, especially with processed foods.
High sodium is a tracking note, not a stand-alone Crohn's trigger.
Protein is useful context, though preparation still controls the final read.

explanationTemplates.good: This starts from a more predictable food category. The ingredient details still matter, and your own history should become the final authority.
explanationTemplates.ehh: This belongs in the test-food middle. The app found some useful cautions, but nothing here automatically means you cannot eat it.
explanationTemplates.ugly: This contains a higher-risk structure or a stronger trigger pattern. The warning is stronger, especially during a flare, but your personal history still matters.
explanationTemplates.unknown: There is not enough reliable food or ingredient information yet. Scan the ingredient label or enter the food name.
